documents.label: A copy of the minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: The form lists these under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' as OR options — any one is sufficient.

parentId.notes: The form says 'provide one from each category' and lists these three under 'For proof of your identity' — any one is sufficient.

address.notes: The form does not list a separate proof-of-address document category. The guardian's current address is collected on the form itself.

childId.notes: The form explicitly says 'For the minor, please provide BOTH of the following' — both the minor's Social Security card and birth certificate are required.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship, if not named on child's birth certificate
Driver's license or another government issued identification card, such as a state ID card
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address, such as a copy of a bank statement, utility bill, insurance statement

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate establishes parentage/authority if the parent is named on it. A separate proof of guardianship is required only if the parent is NOT named on the child's birth certificate — so these two together cover all authority cases as alternatives.

childId.notes: The page lists both 'a copy of your child's birth certificate' and 'a copy of your child's Social Security card' as separate required items under 'all of the following', indicating both are required.

mailingAddress.notes: The page lists two addresses: standard mail (PO Box 9554) and overnight mail (701 Experian Parkway). The standard mail address is the primary mailing address for this request.

documents.label: A document issued by a Federal, State, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare/probation department certifying the protected consumer is in a foster care setting
Copy of a driver's license, motor vehicle administration ID, or any other government-issued identification (child)
Copy of a driver's license, motor vehicle administration ID, or any other government-issued identification (parent)
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (child)
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (parent)

authority.notes: Page explicitly states 'one piece of documentation' from this list is sufficient for proof of authority.

parentId.notes: The page groups parent and child identity documents together under a single 'sufficient proof of identification' requirement (item 3), listing SSN/card, birth certificate, and government-issued ID as options. A birth certificate is listed but in context it is the child's birth certificate (already covering authority); the parent's own identity is satisfied by SSN or government-issued ID. Any one of the listed options is sufficient.

address.notes: The protected consumer freeze instructions do not list any address proof requirement. Address proof is only mentioned as optional (and not required) for a standard adult freeze request.

childId.notes: Item 3 requires 'sufficient proof of identification' for both the parent AND the child. The same document types apply to both: SSN/card, certified birth certificate, or government-issued ID. birth_child appears in both authority and childId because a birth certificate can satisfy both purposes. Any one document is sufficient for the child's identity.

mailingAddress.notes: This address is explicitly designated for the Protected Consumer Freeze (minor/incapacitated adult freeze) in the address-source page. It is distinct from the standard adult freeze address (P.O. Box 160, Woodlyn, PA 19094).
